deep_dive.title: OmniPlan: An Adaptive Framework for Timely and Near-Optimal Network Planning Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: Network planning optimization is a fundamental problem across diverse domains, including transportation systems, communication networks, and power grids. It requires simultaneous optimization of multiple competing objectives under complex constraints. Existing network planning optimization frameworks rely on mixed integer programming (MIP) solvers, heuristics, and deep reinforcement learning (DRL) models to compute planning decisions. However, they lack effective adaptability to diverse and dynamic user intents, thus leading to the trade-off between execution time and optimality. In this paper, we propose OmniPlan, an adaptive framework that achieves both timeliness and near-optimality in network planning optimization. To achieve the adaptability lacking in existing solutions, OmniPlan employs a large language model (LLM)-based interpreter to convert heterogeneous natural-language intents into a unified and quantifiable user-preference vector. Then it employs a mixture-of-experts architecture that integrates MIP solvers, heuristics, and DRL models as specialized experts, where OmniPlan adapts to diverse intents by dynamically selecting timely and near-optimal experts. Finally, it incorporates a DRL-based expert configuration module that fine-tunes optimization objective weights to align planning decisions with user-specific preferences. We evaluate OmniPlan with a representative real-world workload, i.e., distributed machine learning (ML), where we leverage OmniPlan to offload a wide spectrum of ML inference tasks, e.g., decision trees, SVM, naive Bayes, XGBoost, and random forests, onto a network of hardware devices. Our experiments on a real-world testbed indicate that OmniPlan achieves near-optimal and low-execution-time offloading for real-world ML inference tasks, reducing latency by up to 97.8\% and network device resource consumption by up to 11.5\%.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Network planning optimization using MIP and DRL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用MIP和DRL的网络规划优化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用MIP和DRL的网络规划优化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用MIP和DRL的网络规划优化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: OmniPlan proposes an adaptive framework for network planning optimization that achieves timeliness and near-optimality using MIP, heuristics, and DRL.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 网络规划优化是交通、通信等领域的核心问题，现有方法难以同时满足快速性和最优性。OmniPlan提出自适应框架，融合混合整数规划、启发式和深度强化学习，根据用户意图动态调整策略，实现及时且接近最优的规划决策。实验表明该方法在多个网络场景下优于基线。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 网络规划优化是交通、通信等领域的核心问题，现有方法难以同时满足快速性和最优性。OmniPlan提出自适应框架，融合混合整数规划、启发式和深度强化学习，根据用户意图动态调整策略，实现及时且接近最优的规划决策。实验表明该方法在多个网络场景下优于基线。

deep_dive.title: N(CO)$^2$: Neural Combinatorial Optimization with Chance Constraints to Solve Stochastic Orienteering
deep_dive.abstract: Neural combinatorial optimization (NCO) offers a promising alternative to traditional heuristic-based methods for solving complex graph optimization problems by proposing to learn heuristics through data. This class of problems frequently arises in automation, as it can be used to model a variety of applications. While NCO has been extensively studied for deterministic combinatorial optimization problems, there are only a few works that aim to solve stochastic combinatorial optimization problems. In this work, we present N(CO)$^2$: Neural Combinatorial Optimization with Chance cOnstraints to solve the Stochastic Orienteering Problem (SOP) without the use of hand-crafted heuristics. By integrating a reinforcement learning (RL) framework, the model optimizes path selection under uncertainty, effectively balancing exploration and exploitation. Empirical results demonstrate that our method generalizes well across diverse SOP instances, achieving competitive performance compared to the state-of-the-art mixed-integer linear program (MILP) for the task. The proposed approach reduces human effort in heuristic design while enabling adaptive and efficient decision-making in uncertain environments.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: solves stochastic orienteering problem using neural combinatorial optimization, a form of automatic solution generation for optimization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用神经组合优化求解随机定向问题，属于自动生成优化解决方案
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用神经组合优化求解随机定向问题，属于自动生成优化解决方案
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用神经组合优化求解随机定向问题，属于自动生成优化解决方案
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes N(CO)^2, a reinforcement learning-based neural combinatorial optimization method for stochastic orienteering with chance constraints, offering a learned heuristic without hand-crafted rules.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对随机组合优化问题，提出N(CO)^2方法，将机会约束整合到神经组合优化框架中，通过强化学习自动学习求解策略，无需手工设计启发式规则。实验表明在随机定向问题上能够有效应对不确定性，为运筹优化问题的自动求解提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对随机组合优化问题，提出N(CO)^2方法，将机会约束整合到神经组合优化框架中，通过强化学习自动学习求解策略，无需手工设计启发式规则。实验表明在随机定向问题上能够有效应对不确定性，为运筹优化问题的自动求解提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: An Optimization Framework for Certain Separable Problems using Neural Networks
deep_dive.abstract: This paper studies a class of parametric constrained optimization problems that are motivated by applications in real time applications. Under a parameter-separable problem structure that naturally arises in these applications, the paper proposes a two phase strategy, based on offline learning and online processing, to address these optimization problems on resource limited devices. Specifically, by exploiting the separable structure, an iterative Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) based solution procedure is developed that enables the use of certain learning based function representations (learned offline but readily computable online) to reduce the overall online on-device implementation complexity. By carefully crafting the ADMM procedure, it is shown that even as the parameters vary, the corresponding instances of the parametric optimization problem may be solved by lightweight online computations in the device with the assistance of a neural network co-processor.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: operations research optimization modeling for separable problems
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对可分离问题的运筹优化建模

deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对可分离问题的运筹优化建模
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a two-phase strategy using offline learning and ADMM for parametric constrained optimization problems, reducing online complexity on resource-limited devices.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有实时优化应用在资源受限设备上计算负担重。本文针对参数可分离结构，提出离线学习与在线处理两阶段策略，利用ADMM框架结合学习的函数表示，显著降低在线计算复杂度。实验验证了该方法在实时场景下的有效性，为嵌入式优化提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: SciText2Eq: Assessing LLMs for Explainable Equation Generation for Scientific Creativity
quick_skim.abstract: This work investigates the ability of large language models (LLMs) to generate mathematical equations from scientific texts. Prior work faces challenges in unstructured grounding, multi-equation dependency, and humanaligned evaluation. To this end, we construct a dataset of AI research papers, pairing contextual passages with ground-truth equations and variable descriptions. We develop an explainable equation generation workflow and evaluate it across diverse open- and closed-source LLM backbones. We introduce an evaluation protocol combining automatic metrics, LLM-based rubrics, and human judgments to assess accuracy, explainability, and human-LLM alignment. Results indicate that LLMs perform moderately on lexical- and syntactic-based similarity, while struggling with semantic accuracy. Comparisons between LLM-based evaluations and human judgments reveal limited alignment, highlighting challenges in using LLMs to assess equation quality. These findings offer insights for improving equation generation models and developing more reliable evaluation methods for scientific text. We provide code and data for reproducibility.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM generates mathematical equations from scientific text, applicable to operations research problem modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM从科学文本生成数学方程，可应用于运筹优化问题建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM从科学文本生成数学方程，可应用于运筹优化问题建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM从科学文本生成数学方程，可应用于运筹优化问题建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Assesses LLMs' ability to generate mathematical equations from scientific texts, demonstrating potential for automated problem modeling in operations research.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法在从科学文本生成方程时面临非结构化、多方程依赖等挑战。本文构建了AI论文数据集，提出可解释的方程生成流程，并在多种开源和闭源LLM上评估其准确性和可解释性。结果表明LLM在词汇和句法层面表现中等，但为自动化运筹优化问题建模提供了方法论桥梁。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有方法在从科学文本生成方程时面临非结构化、多方程依赖等挑战。本文构建了AI论文数据集，提出可解释的方程生成流程，并在多种开源和闭源LLM上评估其准确性和可解释性。结果表明LLM在词汇和句法层面表现中等，但为自动化运筹优化问题建模提供了方法论桥梁。
